summary: Linked the Mew common item by replacing the 330-line asm/melee/it/items/itmew.s object with a matching C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itmew.c, moving the build to the C object, and marking itmew.c as Matching. The PR added a MewVars special-attributes struct, declared the needed it_8027ADEC prototype, converted internal itmew callbacks and state handlers to static/local scope, and localized the associated float constants in symbols.txt.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itmew.s and added a 158-line C implementation for itmew.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itmew.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itmew.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itmew.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itmew.s.o.

Implemented the Mew ItemStateTable it_803F80C8 in C with three states using callbacks it_802D3E08/it_802D3E2C/it_802D3ED0, it_802D3C50/it_802D3C90/it_802D3C94, and it_802D3D28/it_802D3D6C/it_802D3D8C.
Added MewVars to itCommonItems.h with four f32 fields x0, x4, x8, and xC, matching the special-attributes accesses seen in the asm.
Added an it_8027ADEC declaration in it_2725.h as M2C_UNK it_8027ADEC(M2C_UNK, Item_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, f32).
Converted itmew-internal functions it_802D3B8C through it_802D3ED0 and constants at 0x804DD4B8/0x804DD4BC from global symbols to local symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Kept externally visible entry points it_802D3B18 and it_802D3B6C global while making the state callbacks static in C.
smart_moves: Used a small item-specific special-attributes struct, MewVars, instead of raw offset arithmetic for xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, making velocity and timing parameters clearer while preserving matching layout.
Made state callbacks static/local and updated symbols.txt scope accordingly, reducing accidental global symbol exposure after converting from asm.
Matched hitlag behavior by assigning ip->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and ip->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll in each state setup path that did so in asm.
Preserved compiler-sensitive duplicated Item* temporaries in it_802D3E2C; the author explicitly tried consolidation and left the matched form because removing the duplicate variable did not match.
Added only a conservative M2C_UNK prototype for it_8027ADEC, noting a better typed declaration may be possible but avoiding a broader signature change based on one file.
decomp_lessons: For item files, converting the asm object is not enough: update both configure.py Matching/NonMatching status and obj_files.mk so the build links the C object instead of the asm object.
When decompiling item state tables, callback functions often become file-local static functions; symbols.txt should be adjusted from global to local to match C linkage.
Item special attributes can often be represented as a small per-item struct in itCommonItems.h when multiple offsets are accessed consistently.

Do not over-generalize shared API prototypes from a single usage site; this PR left it_8027ADEC as M2C_UNK even though the author suspected it could be typed as void it_8027ADEC(enum_t, Item_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, f32).
Matched C may need seemingly redundant local variables. In it_802D3E2C, two Item* variables looked identical semantically, but consolidation did not match.
Small float constants from asm sdata2 can become compiler-generated local constants such as @175 and @182 once represented directly as 0.0F and 2.8F in C.
naming_conventions: New item-specific attributes struct was named MewVars and placed in itCommonItems.h.
Unknown struct fields followed offset-style names x0, x4, x8, xC because their semantic names were not established.
Address-based function names it_802D3B18, it_802D3B6C, and static itmew helpers were retained; no semantic renaming was attempted.
State callback prototypes in itmew.c were preceded by address comments such as /* 2D3B8C */.
Local sdata2 constants in symbols.txt used anonymous compiler-style names @175 and @182 rather than exported it_804DD4B8/it_804DD4BC names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the asm implementation with C while preserving exact state ordering and callback layout in ItemStateTable it_803F80C8.
Used HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj) for the HSD_JObj* argument to it_8027ADEC, corresponding to the asm load from gobj->hsd_obj at offset 0x28.
Used GET_ITEM(gobj) to reproduce item pointer loads from gobj->user_data / offset 0x2C.
Used direct float literals 0.0F and 2.8F to replace the former sdata2 globals it_804DD4B8 and it_804DD4BC.
Used HSD_Randi(2) != 0 and an else branch assigning -attr->x4 to match the randomized horizontal velocity sign logic.
Left it_802D3E2C with two Item* locals, ip_arg and ip, because attempts to consolidate them failed to match.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice.
Author note: it_8027ADEC could probably be declared as void it_8027ADEC(enum_t, Item_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, f32), but the PR avoided changing it based on use in a single file.
Author note: it_802D3E2C matched but looked improvable because it used two identical item pointers.
Author follow-up: consolidating the two variables in it_802D3E2C did not work; the author suspected it may have a similar form to it_802D3BE0 but kept the already-matched version.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that typed it_8027ADEC more specifically than M2C_UNK.
Search for later renames or semantic field names for MewVars x0 x4 x8 xC.
Find item decomp PRs that preserve duplicate Item* locals to match codegen.
Search for adjacent Pokemon item links such as itmetamon, itpippi, ittogepy, itcerebi, ithitodeman, and itlucky for reusable item-state-table patterns.
Check whether it_802D3E2C was later refactored after matching or kept in the two-variable form.
